# AI in Finance Daily — 2026-07-10

*Machines are coming for the portfolio itself — and the quants are bleeding.*

## 🔎 The lead

**JPMorgan built AI agents that beat the 60/40 portfolio in backtests** [108](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitgFBVV95cUxQeERVOTV0M2h4M1RqZThDemF4cTZrb29qbndmazlsN2lUZEFDTE1GV3Mza3pfbjF5THhkbFhyblZRc2JTcVpLZkFMMU9rX1U3LTBqWmtESjRITWgwN0hybElacS1OcUFndkNIMkNlOVk2UklBOFhUUi1tMmh1czNJdW9malh4cVI3bU1ES1VHUnZGaDRyVDdqRHBGUXhSWno0WDZOWU9sNFc3eWZMT3hIX3Z4eDQ1dw?oc=5), and is reportedly testing a model that can move client money without asking first [107](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifEFVX3lxTE1fclk5MDJTY2pOMy0yaDF2OHNoeUM2Ul95WmRzaERoTjB5MVFtWjRyMmdMODd5OWNJWGh2a0hDQnZ2c2hOdzF0c3BLelRKdkVxaTlYd2FLYi1EYmZFTmlJWENRZU9vN0p3NjJSQjN5bWQ3blpvZ3RPcFRiVE0?oc=5) `(that framing is from a crypto outlet — verify against the primary)`. This is the clearest signal yet that the biggest US bank sees autonomous portfolio management as a product, not a research toy. It lands in the same week Korean media report regulators worrying about inconsistent AI financial advice [97](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijgFBVV95cUxNSDJoQ3doS1o3ZTdDMEJ0OHVmQ2pNRk83cWN0bTNGQTVXYkduTTVDbTZnLVhmMGR4S0kxUHVTQS1wNzJfRkl1YnhPUkhmZGwyZ0gwS3JPdjFuSFNINEZzSnlWQ1pNeVBhbm1ZeWNqS0dpZFBOY190ajh0alNDbXNlMVpoS09ZX2hQR2NnazhR?oc=5) — the product is arriving faster than the rulebook. **So what:** backtest-beating agents + real-money autonomy is exactly the combination supervisors (MAS, FCA — see Monday's brief) are now writing assurance regimes for. Whoever ships compliant autonomy first wins a very large prize.

## ⚡ The big picture

- **Quants just had their worst run since August** — an AI selloff drove the damage [25](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxQM0g1X0N2bzdzWFJhT1FwMGl4VTMxV0diWWkxaXVuT18xNEF4dXhVeGtneG45akpzTmQyUjlkTE9hOGE2aVlwWFY3NmNpMEpHbWYyMWFIQ0NQcm0tUDhPTzh0NW5aLWZPcEh2NE0teGdhVG1EdGVWSGVFRXpRMmtDLXdmbXBEV2hNb3RZWElELTY1VnplZXY5bEtMODAtU3diLTdTV3NyU1RrMkZjdi1IYw?oc=5), and Goldman says the AI trade reversal is squeezing hedge fund returns broadly [30](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiwgFBVV95cUxPU1NlaVRMYVY5VDNZbEJucFVHYUVTMzB5WTdwX3VzVEY4aXU5SXNKeHpONHVwcVZUbFlXbDl4ZzlnQkYwbzdHTFR2UE5yVm1VTTRCZE5WekZUU1ExZjFraHA3RmFnVXR5TjFOSHVNV2dmMnZ4WGlOWWh2SzBfZ21QZHBTdjQwc3lWRmtPN3hqQWtZMUMxWGtHdGVfTjUwYVVoZURRakx2Z0Etc1J1SUNjU0ZFVUx0Nlh2NmhtVjhKRlRxZw?oc=5).
- **BlackRock is talking both sides** — CIO Rick Rieder says the firm trimmed and rebalanced its most AI-exposed holdings [59](https://news.google.?oc=5), while its strategists insist capex is stable for 2–3 more years [43](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiswFBVV95cUxONWtmWElhRE4xMjU3X0VvX0IyYXB2dVNLb1JCT1VwNmoyQTdIS3QzTl9XUzc4a3NTUE02MFFTcVhxNHpDOC04M25pWjhnb2pzbXZOS3dQUUttOHdaT2MyRjA1cmhsZ0NZWF94clNnR29scmpZeWt3aHlXQTZHSEdCZElvQmViSFkwRGdhQV8xMmw4VUJUZWxmbkg1QUZlbUVBTC1FQndJUXZETVZBV0J6d3NDYw?oc=5) and the firm launches a Nasdaq-100 ETF to ride the rally [75](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi-_?oc=5). Hedge, meet marketing.
- **The SEC hired a Citadel alum to advise on AI** [9](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxPZTZaZV8tQXlfanJVV19rLW9qcHVvYWdlRjlna3NzYWpESDR5QXVFVk56aHplRGV2dVdWMGd4NmRCOGVkOFBxcjZmdkhDazBnb0RYOVpMY2NtN2RsYVBmaUtlWGVhUkQ3YmpmNlZDalRRRm5DamZQa25SR0ZCYndpMUtITVE5eE1ZVlAyRGN4YS1PME9kbDlvdGJ3VnBHTFpqSE9QbTN3MnQ0RUE?oc=5) — the regulator is buying industry expertise rather than growing it.
- **HSBC expanded its Google Cloud partnership to scale AI** across the bank [105](https://news.google.?oc=5).
- **OpenAI acquired Hiro**, signaling a push into AI-driven personal finance [99](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMikgFBVV95cUxNelR0MkpoT1BlZEhoQmplZXFMUTZWUDZkby04QnN1TDVSMk9fd0tiai0tem8wUUt0NVJhQW91Q3lPXy15TE1UR05HODZnR1B5cTlaNUhmNlhOamgzUTVyM0VhYlgtSnFNX2xTVTJNb2hSX3pVUWRnYTBxem95cWF0SnQ4Zk5zMXRiN1FwMGNZWnJVZw?oc=5) — the model vendors are moving downstream into financial products.

## 💼 Asset management

- **Goldman became Taiwan's #1 foreign broker on an AI-quant push** [54](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxOdzRGSFQ3UFg1b1hZTlI4alp2b2FCY0gwTE1TeDVkaFRUeVkxRVRRUlhxSGZxMzdIUDAwNHR5QnpOTXhKdURRNENRMVVTbHRaTHEtcGsxSjV3S2dIYzZxbVVDNWRxUjlSZGhldTlNVV9XYWhMZi0zekRSN2ZlaFBpOXRWOXhpQ0IzQkRPb2owYlNRcm5WTHhjdVZaOXE3QWFyakJlLWhrRzV3Wm9XeWRUcw?oc=5) — AI execution as market-share weapon, not just cost saver.
- **Lazard's asset-management CEO is rebuilding the firm around hiring and AI** [68](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiwwFBVV95cUxQcFFSWUNoV2NtaktHTDYyTEtsS0sySDZJbWRBUG8yVkNyd2N5X2gtVk5WZ2NWcXJyUTktMlUzYUtnejBnUmVCdkc5UFNaMHhZaC1OZmxPQjE0Ulc3d2cyZ1NTeGpTbUZza3FFdGs4bVhfb1NtU1JXdjBVREs1aUFCTTI1dHZfbXU1S2xpOXZLUTA4UWh3dGV2MGNhY1pPYnlMSVV4S1RDZmtoNGNwMndNeHMxRFZTTUpaa0pjdy05eldpaFU?oc=5).
- **Dispersion keeps widening:** Appaloosa's AI-memory bets drove a 32% H1 [51](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi-?oc=5), while Bronte Capital sank 9% after missing the AI chip boom [98](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMivAFBVV95cUxNbUIwX093dEJKM1pGSWFta1NtcTZlOFBTa2Z2R0tDWkYyR3hvZUV0UGJUcVZQbERMVlloczJqYldMZG9uRjNuU2dWcjhjTHhSenNEcTVNUW1sRjAwNnNjZk44M3RkMG1STXFuNWtuTEdBdmxsbTRGWWRYTWNRSEwyejF0VDd2OUd4R2ZTUlpPZFdtQTBNZW1YQmQ2QVNmanNGQVJaWkdxeWV3MElRLWpYRWRXTHFxc09yRDZkQQ?oc=5). Owning or missing one theme now defines the year.
- **Citadel is all-in on the narrative** — Ken Griffin talked AI, US-China and data centers [11](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixAFBVV95cUxNZUpvYzYwN2dETl9taFJXV0xGR1NabzA5Y1MtZ3d5cndUc1FRWnF2TzhzeVQtSnVyVXJuS3FkeTZjOVJOVjFfYkVHcndGZUpNcnY1VHpTQUhHUDNrc1ZaTjVjNFp3RXBoMEFZSlVuNkNMc1FjVUNLLXdiRjZyUjBsdk96N0lBMzVycmQ0WWpWX01RbmJyYWxhTGRXNWlqazNsN3l6b1hZSmlNZHRuTDBuejVFQkF6dUxORTI5Y3FQUDFHZTFB?oc=5), and Citadel Securities calls AI a boost to American "hustle culture" [65](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxPZkxEdG1rRHU2QVJfN0FpUDh0ZTg3MjFxN2NoWjczYjhlb3JuQmxzRnJ0dGVMZkJFR2RWZU5BMUlQUmJ2QjQyNWVxREdweXpBRHRkUHEwdGNaZG1XVU5MQ1NzTnIyclo4Z2RFaFAwVEd1Rm11b19hWUszTHNvOVRLZXQ1OVM4b0w4aVU3ZllwY0JlV2RyeWVYdVdHRkxYcURtTG5QbzVIcW5tamxyYnQ2VA?oc=5).
- **A startup wave is gunning for the quants' secret sauce** — Business Insider reports new AI firms automating alpha discovery itself [78](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimwFBVV95cUxPT0NkY2VvcThqd0hnYVdHSGg5OWJ3bDRlUjN6cGlaVEIyX2JVU0wydzVQZDlkd3lDbXdiRVdXZVNHeF84OWo4Smg1UjRxX1JGRFFzZnV1ZmNtSU94VFN5LUlWRkJ0SE5oWHhHbktlRzNEUU5STDB2dHVYNjVlU2t1dlBycVh2ZXVhZHBiNmpSVGxZZ1NHR1RJQ2tIOA?oc=5).
- **India's SBI Mutual Fund deployed AI for trading surveillance and research** [89](https://news.google.?oc=5); Australia's HeirWealth shipped an MCP server so advisers can query client wealth data through AI assistants [91](https://www.finextra.com/pressarticle/110384/heirwealth-switches-on-mcp-server?utm_medium=rssfinextra&utm_source=finextrafeed).

## 🏦 Around the industry

- **Funding:** EDX Markets raised a $76M Series C on its trust-bank bid [100](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMihgFBVV95cUxQYW9oVjYxQmNTUFBKTHJDcGgtSGhBT2k3dGNVWEpTTHNialp4TWxqRENpM3BLdmxIOEpKanRISHRHNWhxYlNsdUt5dnBmOWNBV2V4aTZpNTc4ckVndC1rVVBmcmRuT0k3UlF2RklmaXY2LVRETTFLeUhJYWhaS1oxYVB1SWtDUQ?oc=5); Banyan Software invested in WIZE, a wealth platform serving 120+ institutions across 27 countries [46](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijgFBVV95cUxOeDRWQW94cDVFc1hpbkZIQjZjbDNFQ3pJQmNXQ0I5RWw1cGlzU3J1ZldycTFnQjFSN1N1blpjLXpDNGZqU3U3MWdxZW9kUDAzb2ljUXBBWWVvSGRENVRueXJLalJqUWRyaWxnVFIySDlScm1Jb3hXa1ZuMGtyMWVJb1VCY19vRlBSaFp3cEpn?oc=5).
- **Regulation:** South Korea's financial regulator wants AI deployed to fight AI-aided attacks [101](https://news.google.?oc=5); Seoul press reports concern over inconsistent AI financial advice [97](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijgFBVV95cUxNSDJoQ3doS1o3ZTdDMEJ0OHVmQ2pNRk83cWN0bTNGQTVXYkduTTVDbTZnLVhmMGR4S0kxUHVTQS1wNzJfRkl1YnhPUkhmZGwyZ0gwS3JPdjFuSFNINEZzSnlWQ1pNeVBhbm1ZeWNqS0dpZFBOY190ajh0alNDbXNlMVpoS09ZX2hQR2NnazhR?oc=5); the Eurogroup tied fiscal discipline to AI and digital-finance resilience [109](https://news.google.?oc=5); a new US "Aging With AI Act" will study chatbots' impact on seniors [104](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMikAFBVV95cUxQWFMxVHhDLVRPQkVVRFpZcTBzUklJLTNUV0FlZDNaQndJRVZtX1I0cVRybkFNT2NsSjdEZ2wwZkRmb3lab3V4NEE1a3J2T0g1MmRYcmxIT1BhNXR1cnVZdXRLNzkwamJldG9qeVdNbzV3enVWeTZoUVNGTjY1OHhsNk4zc0VqZnJRMXBxcThIdzQ?oc=5).
- **Chips underneath it all:** UBS says monthly memory-chip sales hit a record, with shortages seen persisting to 2028 [103](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimAFBVV95cUxNTGJFNVFSSU5DcEh1RWxuTFNaUkhiT3lGN2dRVTFPY21GV1ZzdEtyUWs4SVRuZTQ3b0pFbldQaTR6cGtwX1owOFNKSTZSbTVRWXFSTE9OandmQy1aSm5uV2pYdkJ0RE1QWl92Z3hLZTBGYWNTbE9RSVhkSEtMcE1WSEVoMzAxckZja2o2RlJmeTZROGJHVXVJaA?oc=5) `(thin sourcing — syndicated)`.

## 📚 Research corner

- **XALPHA** — a memory-driven AI "quant researcher" that goes from hypothesis to alpha code [90](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08332v1). The academic version of what those secret-sauce startups [78](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimwFBVV95cUxPT0NkY2VvcThqd0hnYVdHSGg5OWJ3bDRlUjN6cGlaVEIyX2JVU0wydzVQZDlkd3lDbXdiRVdXZVNHeF84OWo4Smg1UjRxX1JGRFFzZnV1ZmNtSU94VFN5LUlWRkJ0SE5oWHhHbktlRzNEUU5STDB2dHVYNjVlU2t1dlBycVh2ZXVhZHBiNmpSVGxZZ1NHR1RJQ2tIOA?oc=5) are selling.
- **Can we trust LLM logic?** A graph-based framework quantifies reasoning uncertainty [93](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08017v1) — directly useful for model-risk teams validating AI advice.

## 📡 Radar

- **Alpha discovery is being commoditized** — an arXiv pipeline [90](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.08332v1), a startup wave [78](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimwFBVV95cUxPT0NkY2VvcThqd0hnYVdHSGg5OWJ3bDRlUjN6cGlaVEIyX2JVU0wydzVQZDlkd3lDbXdiRVdXZVNHeF84OWo4Smg1UjRxX1JGRFFzZnV1ZmNtSU94VFN5LUlWRkJ0SE5oWHhHbktlRzNEUU5STDB2dHVYNjVlU2t1dlBycVh2ZXVhZHBiNmpSVGxZZ1NHR1RJQ2tIOA?oc=5) and Goldman's AI-quant share grab [54](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxOdzRGSFQ3UFg1b1hZTlI4alp2b2FCY0gwTE1TeDVkaFRUeVkxRVRRUlhxSGZxMzdIUDAwNHR5QnpOTXhKdURRNENRMVVTbHRaTHEtcGsxSjV3S2dIYzZxbVVDNWRxUjlSZGhldTlNVV9XYWhMZi0zekRSN2ZlaFBpOXRWOXhpQ0IzQkRPb2owYlNRcm5WTHhjdVZaOXE3QWFyakJlLWhrRzV3Wm9XeWRUcw?oc=5) all point the same way: systematic edge is getting cheaper to manufacture, worse to own.
- **Previously:** Monday flagged discretionary AI winners vs. quant losers. Thread confirmed and extended — quants' worst run since August [25](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxQM0g1X0N2bzdzWFJhT1FwMGl4VTMxV0diWWkxaXVuT18xNEF4dXhVeGtneG45akpzTmQyUjlkTE9hOGE2aVlwWFY3NmNpMEpHbWYyMWFIQ0NQcm0tUDhPTzh0NW5aLWZPcEh2NE0teGdhVG1EdGVWSGVFRXpRMmtDLXdmbXBEV2hNb3RZWElELTY1VnplZXY5bEtMODAtU3diLTdTV3NyU1RrMkZjdi1IYw?oc=5), Goldman blaming the AI unwind [30](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiwgFBVV95cUxPU1NlaVRMYVY5VDNZbEJucFVHYUVTMzB5WTdwX3VzVEY4aXU5SXNKeHpONHVwcVZUbFlXbDl4ZzlnQkYwbzdHTFR2UE5yVm1VTTRCZE5WekZUU1ExZjFraHA3RmFnVXR5TjFOSHVNV2dmMnZ4WGlOWWh2SzBfZ21QZHBTdjQwc3lWRmtPN3hqQWtZMUMxWGtHdGVfTjUwYVVoZURRakx2Z0Etc1J1SUNjU0ZFVUx0Nlh2NmhtVjhKRlRxZw?oc=5).
- **Autonomy is outrunning assurance** — JPMorgan's money-moving agents [107](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifEFVX3lxTE1fclk5MDJTY2pOMy0yaDF2OHNoeUM2Ul95WmRzaERoTjB5MVFtWjRyMmdMODd5OWNJWGh2a0hDQnZ2c2hOdzF0c3BLelRKdkVxaTlYd2FLYi1EYmZFTmlJWENRZU9vN0p3NjJSQjN5bWQ3blpvZ3RPcFRiVE0?oc=5), adviser-facing MCP access [91](https://www.finextra.com/pressarticle/110384/heirwealth-switches-on-mcp-server?utm_medium=rssfinextra&utm_source=finextrafeed) and Korean advice-consistency worries [97](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijgFBVV95cUxNSDJoQ3doS1o3ZTdDMEJ0OHVmQ2pNRk83cWN0bTNGQTVXYkduTTVDbTZnLVhmMGR4S0kxUHVTQS1wNzJfRkl1YnhPUkhmZGwyZ0gwS3JPdjFuSFNINEZzSnlWQ1pNeVBhbm1ZeWNqS0dpZFBOY190ajh0alNDbXNlMVpoS09ZX2hQR2NnazhR?oc=5) in one 48-hour window. Agentic finance is now a live supervisory problem, not a 2027 one.
- **AI-fabricated finance news is a market risk** — one report claims AI engines invented $36B in fictional hedge-fund collapses [13](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMieEFVX3lxTE50NnJwOUQyZWo2MXVMY1BDVUJXRWh4N05zQmdIQkJlV2d1OFUtYlhFVUs1eGhRWGNKeldObnotLWpwbVNONHhQYWdsNVVSUmdZTTV6bVY2QmlDUDdXdmZFU0hCcmh4Sm5BVGpUN2FhV2FWa2lPeE9kMw?oc=5) `(thin sourcing)`. If true, fund PR and IR teams have a new tail risk.

## 🗒️ Fine print

- No briefs published Jul 8–9: the automated runs failed on missing API credits (collection ran; synthesis didn't). Continuity resumes today.
- The JPMorgan "moves money without asking" characterization [107](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMifEFVX3lxTE1fclk5MDJTY2pOMy0yaDF2OHNoeUM2Ul95WmRzaERoTjB5MVFtWjRyMmdMODd5OWNJWGh2a0hDQnZ2c2hOdzF0c3BLelRKdkVxaTlYd2FLYi1EYmZFTmlJWENRZU9vN0p3NjJSQjN5bWQ3blpvZ3RPcFRiVE0?oc=5) comes from a crypto outlet paraphrasing Bloomberg's report [108](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitgFBVV95cUxQeERVOTV0M2h4M1RqZThDemF4cTZrb29qbndmazlsN2lUZEFDTE1GV3Mza3pfbjF5THhkbFhyblZRc2JTcVpLZkFMMU9rX1U3LTBqWmtESjRITWgwN0hybElacS1OcUFndkNIMkNlOVk2UklBOFhUUi1tMmh1czNJdW9malh4cVI3bU1ES1VHUnZGaDRyVDdqRHBGUXhSWno0WDZOWU9sNFc3eWZMT3hIX3Z4eDQ1dw?oc=5) — read the primary before repeating it.
- Man Group "betting big on AI tokens" [41](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiogFBVV95cUxOemlrQTRXR3NhSy00U1oyRV9OY2M4OHpUd0RJTFBmaUh0YkdiektBX043ak1CWDhRbmlRUHlMXy13UThmeFVMdU92c0J5SFYzSG9DX1FWWmQ3TlpfM1ZxeXF5anVvaUNhX0Z1bHJQeVktQldDNmpnZU1rVFpvU2J5eE1CdlVwNVQxLWZHSEs1YndFdGZha1d6REZ0bVcyUVljanc?oc=5) and a $21.8B AI-in-AM market forecast [81](https://news.google.?oc=5) are PR/thin sources — not relied on above.
- BlackRock's trim [59](https://news.google.?oc=5) vs. its bullish capex call [43](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiswFBVV95cUxONWtmWElhRE4xMjU3X0VvX0IyYXB2dVNLb1JCT1VwNmoyQTdIS3QzTl9XUzc4a3NTUE02MFFTcVhxNHpDOC04M25pWjhnb2pzbXZOS3dQUUttOHdaT2MyRjA1cmhsZ0NZWF94clNnR29scmpZeWt3aHlXQTZHSEdCZElvQmViSFkwRGdhQV8xMmw4VUJUZWxmbkg1QUZlbUVBTC1FQndJUXZETVZBV0J6d3NDYw?oc=5) may be portfolio vs. house-view nuance rather than contradiction; primary interviews would settle it.
